quick_skim.title: STRIDE: Strategic Trajectory Reasoning via Discriminative Estimation for Verifiable Reinforcement Learning
quick_skim.abstract: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) has become an effective post-training paradigm for improving the reasoning abilities of large language models. However, existing RLVR methods typically rely on final-answer correctness to assign trajectory-level rewards, providing sparse supervision and treating all tokens uniformly regardless of their actual contribution to reasoning. Although recent studies introduce intermediate signals such as process rewards, high-entropy tokens, and semantic uncertainty, these signals are often not inherently verifiable and may fail to distinguish beneficial strategic patterns from harmful ones. To address this limitation, we propose STRIDE (Strategic Trajectory Reasoning with Discriminative Estimation), a fine-grained RLVR framework that derives strategic reasoning supervision from verifiable outcomes. STRIDE contrasts successful and failed trajectories within each response group to estimate the outcome-discriminative preference of each $n$-gram strategic pattern, and further combines this signal with reasoning saliency entropy to identify decision-relevant strategic patterns. These patterns are assigned differentiated advantage values during RL optimization, enabling more precise credit assignment while preserving the verifiability of RLVR. Extensive experiments demonstrate that STRIDE consistently improves reasoning performance across diverse models, tasks, and extended settings, including VLMs and agent-based systems.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes token-level discriminative rewards for RL-based post-training of LLM reasoning, applicable to agent training
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出用于大语言模型推理后训练的令牌级判别式奖励，适用于智能体训练
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出用于大语言模型推理后训练的令牌级判别式奖励，适用于智能体训练
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出用于大语言模型推理后训练的令牌级判别式奖励，适用于智能体训练
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: STRIDE improves RL with verifiable rewards by estimating token-level contributions to final answers and distinguishing beneficial from harmful strategies, providing denser supervision for post-training reasoning agents.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: STRIDE针对现有RLVR方法仅依赖最终答案正确性、忽略令牌贡献差异的问题，设计了一种判别式估计方法为每个令牌分配细粒度奖励。该方法能区分策略性有益令牌与有害令牌，为推理过程提供更密集的验证信号。实验表明，这种监督方式能有效提升大语言模型的推理能力，并有望应用于自主智能体的后训练。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: STRIDE针对现有RLVR方法仅依赖最终答案正确性、忽略令牌贡献差异的问题，设计了一种判别式估计方法为每个令牌分配细粒度奖励。该方法能区分策略性有益令牌与有害令牌，为推理过程提供更密集的验证信号。实验表明，这种监督方式能有效提升大语言模型的推理能力，并有望应用于自主智能体的后训练。

quick_skim.title: Retrievable Gradients: Continual Post-Training Without Cumulative Weight Drift
quick_skim.abstract: Continual post-training enables models to absorb emerging knowledge after deployment, but repeatedly updating shared parameters can accumulate weight drift, potentially causing catastrophic forgetting and degrading general capabilities. Retrieval-augmented generation avoids such parameter drift, yet often lacks the depth of parametric knowledge integration. In this paper, we propose ReGrad (Retrievable Gradients), a new paradigm that treats gradients as retrievable units of knowledge. ReGrad pre-computes document-specific gradients offline, stores them in an indexed Gradient Bank, and retrieves only query-relevant gradients at inference time for temporary weight adaptation. However, raw language-modeling gradients are optimized for token-level document reconstruction rather than for query-driven knowledge use. We therefore introduce a bi-level meta-learning objective that reshapes document-derived gradients into generalizable adaptation signals for downstream tasks. Experiments across general and domain-specific settings show that \textsc{ReGrad} outperforms CPT and RAG baselines, enabling scalable and reversible parametric knowledge injection without accumulating weight drift.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: continual post-training method using retrievable gradients to prevent weight drift
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用可检索梯度进行持续后训练以防止权重漂移的方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用可检索梯度进行持续后训练以防止权重漂移的方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用可检索梯度进行持续后训练以防止权重漂移的方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes ReGrad, a continual post-training paradigm that stores document-specific gradients in a bank and retrieves relevant gradients at inference for temporary weight adaptation, avoiding catastrophic forgetting.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对持续后训练中权重漂移导致灾难性遗忘的问题，提出 ReGrad 方法：将文档相关梯度离线存储于梯度库，推理时检索仅与查询相关的梯度进行临时权重调整。该方法在保持模型泛化能力的同时吸收新知识，为自主智能体的持续后训练提供了新范式。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对持续后训练中权重漂移导致灾难性遗忘的问题，提出 ReGrad 方法：将文档相关梯度离线存储于梯度库，推理时检索仅与查询相关的梯度进行临时权重调整。该方法在保持模型泛化能力的同时吸收新知识，为自主智能体的持续后训练提供了新范式。

quick_skim.title: ADaPT: Token-Level Decoupling for Efficient Large Reasoning Models
quick_skim.abstract: Large reasoning models rely on long chain-of-thought to achieve strong performance, but applying such reasoning uniformly incurs high computational cost. Existing efficiency-oriented methods attempt to shorten or mix reasoning strategies, yet often degrade reasoning capability. We identify the root cause as sequence-level coupling between efficiency incentives and correctness optimization, which implicitly penalizes long but correct reasoning trajectories. To address this issue, we propose Adaptive Dual-Process Thinking (ADaPT), a token-level dual-process framework that explicitly decouples efficiency and correctness signals during training. ADaPT introduces a mode-selection token to control fast and slow reasoning, applying efficiency-related rewards exclusively to this token to avoid penalizing correct long reasoning while encouraging efficiency when appropriate. Moreover, ADaPT enables precise and continuous control over the efficiency-performance trade-off at inference time: by adjusting the generation probability of the mode-selection token, a single trained model can smoothly move along the efficiency-performance Pareto frontier. Extensive experiments demonstrate that ADaPT significantly reduces inference cost while maintaining strong reasoning performance across multiple benchmarks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: training framework that decouples efficiency and correctness for LLM reasoning, a post-training technique for efficient agents
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 分离效率与正确性的训练框架，用于高效推理的后训练技术，可应用于智能体
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 分离效率与正确性的训练框架，用于高效推理的后训练技术，可应用于智能体
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 分离效率与正确性的训练框架，用于高效推理的后训练技术，可应用于智能体
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: ADaPT introduces a token-level dual-process training method to enable LLMs to adaptively switch between fast and slow reasoning, reducing cost without sacrificing correctness.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: ADaPT指出当前高效推理方法因序列级耦合误罚正确但长的推理轨迹，提出在训练中显式解耦效率与正确信号。通过引入模式选择令牌，模型可学会自适应切换快速与慢速推理。效率相关奖励仅作用于模式令牌，避免干扰正确性优化。实验显示模型能以更低计算量保持高性能，为智能体的自适应后训练提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: ADaPT指出当前高效推理方法因序列级耦合误罚正确但长的推理轨迹，提出在训练中显式解耦效率与正确信号。通过引入模式选择令牌，模型可学会自适应切换快速与慢速推理。效率相关奖励仅作用于模式令牌，避免干扰正确性优化。实验显示模型能以更低计算量保持高性能，为智能体的自适应后训练提供了新思路。
